run_daily_report.py.reason: Generates the daily report in the scheduled task.

git_push.py.reason: Pushes generated reports in the scheduled task.

daily_task_win.py.reason: Windows-native WeChat sync step used by the scheduled task.

path_utils.py.reason: Shared path resolver and config loader.

log.py.reason: Centralised logging configuration replacing print() across modules.

wechat_sync.py.reason: Active Markdown-to-WeChat draft publisher for the daily report.

auto_generate_ai.py.reason: Backward-compatible facade re-exporting from llm_client and report_utils. Used by run_daily_report.py.

llm_client.py.reason: LLM API multi-provider rotation logic extracted from auto_generate_ai.py.

report_utils.py.reason: Report output utilities (path building, saving, link validation, network detection) extracted from auto_generate_ai.py.

report_prompting.py.reason: Prompt and language configuration helpers used by the active report generation path.

report_history_extraction.py.reason: Content extraction helpers for updating paper, project, and news history from generated reports.

arxiv_fetcher.py.reason: Paper fetcher used by the active report generation path.

news_fetcher.py.reason: News fetcher used by the active report generation path.

robust_fetch.py.reason: Shared HTTP retry helper used by active fetchers.

history_db.py.reason: SQLite history state used by active report generation.

history_tracker.py.reason: JSON-based history tracker replaced by history_db.py (SQLite). Zero imports found across active source.

daily_task.py.reason: Older non-Windows workflow; scheduled task uses daily_task_win.py.

auto_generate_draft.py.reason: Early draft generator; active generation uses run_daily_report.py and auto_generate_ai.py.

run_all_tasks.bat.reason: Older batch entry; active scheduler uses run_daily_schedule.bat.

run_daily.sh.reason: Shell entry not used by the Windows scheduled task.

run_auto.sh.reason: Shell entry not used by the Windows scheduled task.

install_daily_task.sh.reason: Legacy shell installer; Windows scheduler setup uses setup_windows_task.ps1.
